title: Public API audit
text: inventory intentionally keeps existing exports. Categories describe current positioning, removal decisions.

title: Public API audit
text: spliv, sp_ltz, sp_uci, conley_ltz, conley_uci, spliv_pattern, spliv_eval_pattern, spliv_sensitivity_path, spliv_tipping_point, bpe_design, bpe_eval_subset, bpe_validate_design, plot_sp_sensitivity, plot.spliv_sensitivity_path, plot.plausexog_fit, plot_conley_sensitivity, sp_prior_ltz, sp_sensitivity_ltz_normal, sp_sensitivity_uci_support, sp_sensitivity_ltz_uniform01_as_normal, iv_inst_names. functions documented uniform/patterned sensitivity, path, BPE, plotting workflows. parameter return-value documentation generated roxygen blocks R/ shared aliases noted generated .Rd files.

title: Public API audit
text: conley_prior_ltz, conley_sensitivity_ltz_normal, conley_sensitivity_uci_support, conley_sensitivity_ltz_uniform01_as_normal, plausexog_iv, demean_fixest, demean_lfe, embed_prior_into_full_Z. exports preserve prior API provide thin name-compatible wrapper; new code prefer primary functions described .

title: Public API audit
text: plausexog, bpe_explore_subsets, bpe_find_subset, estimate_gamma_zero_first_stage. emit explicit deprecation exploratory warnings applicable. particular, subgroup search confirmatory BPE: use pre-specified bpe_design() bpe_validate_design() confirmatory work.

title: Public API audit
text: None current exports removed release. Future API review may consider demean_*, embed_prior_into_full_Z, low-level sp_* wrappers internal status downstream usage known; decision deferred.

title: Public API audit
text: exported functions roxygen parameter/return documentation directly explicit shared @rdname block. Runnable synthetic examples cover primary workflows package README getting-started vignette; deprecated wrappers tested warning behavior. generated manual source truth roxygen2::roxygenise().

title: Getting started with spliv
text: spliv() estimator accepts usual IV formula y ~ X | Z. baseline fit delta = 0 ordinary IV calculation, positive delta allows bounded uniform direct effect.
code: baseline <- spliv(f, d, method = "uci", delta = 0, vcov = "hc1") baseline$estimates #>          term    conf.low  conf.high #> 1 (Intercept) -0.18384632 0.09088447 #> 2           x  1.29870349 1.84425108 #> 3           w -0.01990612 0.28613695 uniform <- spliv(f, d, method = "uci", delta = 0.20, vcov = "hc1",                  grid = list(steps = 9)) uniform$estimates #>          term   conf.low conf.high #> 1 (Intercept) -0.2487226 0.1183848 #> 2           x  0.8961868 2.3416858 #> 3           w -0.2070957 0.4255989

title: Getting started with spliv
text: Patterns explicit objects substantive rationale. direct effect allowed larger synthetic exposure w larger.
code: pattern <- spliv_pattern(   name = "Exposure pattern", pattern = ~ w,   rationale = "The alternative channel is stronger at higher exposure.",   variables_used = "w", pattern_type = "theory_defined",   normalize = "max_abs" ) spliv_eval_pattern(pattern, d)[1:5] #> [1]  0.083746179  0.035128314  0.380408513  0.233287898 -0.001435305 patterned_uci <- spliv(f, d, method = "uci", delta = 0.20, vcov = "hc1",                        violation_pattern = pattern, grid = list(steps = 9)) patterned_ltz <- spliv(f, d, method = "ltz", delta = 0.20, vcov = "hc1",                        violation_pattern = pattern) patterned_uci$estimates #>          term    conf.low  conf.high #> 1 (Intercept) -0.18444170 0.09195752 #> 2           x  1.29090596 1.85535775 #> 3           w -0.02698832 0.30162026 patterned_ltz$estimates #>          term    estimate  std.error    conf.low  conf.high #> 1 (Intercept) -0.04648093 0.07008608 -0.18384711 0.09088526 #> 2           x  1.57147728 0.13918270  1.29868421 1.84427036 #> 3           w  0.13311542 0.07874347 -0.02121894 0.28744978

title: Getting started with spliv
text: Paths make sensitivity grid explicit. tipping point reported supplied interval contains zero grid value.
code: path <- spliv_sensitivity_path(   f, d, method = "uci", delta_grid = seq(0, 0.30, by = 0.05),   vcov = "hc1", violation_pattern = pattern ) head(path) #>          term delta method    estimate    conf_low  conf_high contains_zero #> 1 (Intercept)  0.00    uci -0.04648093 -0.18384632 0.09088447          TRUE #> 2           x  0.00    uci  1.57147728  1.29870349 1.84425108         FALSE #> 3           w  0.00    uci  0.13311542 -0.01990612 0.28613695          TRUE #> 4 (Intercept)  0.05    uci -0.04648093 -0.18393310 0.09109067          TRUE #> 5           x  0.05    uci  1.57147728  1.29713579 1.84664606         FALSE #> 6           w  0.05    uci  0.13311542 -0.02147632 0.28982073          TRUE #>       pattern_name   pattern_type violation_pattern_used scale_instrument nobs #> 1 Exposure pattern theory_defined                   TRUE      residual_sd  240 #> 2 Exposure pattern theory_defined                   TRUE      residual_sd  240 #> 3 Exposure pattern theory_defined                   TRUE      residual_sd  240 #> 4 Exposure pattern theory_defined                   TRUE      residual_sd  240 #> 5 Exposure pattern theory_defined                   TRUE      residual_sd  240 #> 6 Exposure pattern theory_defined                   TRUE      residual_sd  240 #>   se theta_min theta_max baseline_estimate baseline_conf_low baseline_conf_high #> 1 NA      0.00      0.00       -0.04648093       -0.18384632         0.09088447 #> 2 NA      0.00      0.00        1.57147728        1.29870349         1.84425108 #> 3 NA      0.00      0.00        0.13311542       -0.01990612         0.28613695 #> 4 NA     -0.05      0.05       -0.04648093       -0.18384632         0.09088447 #> 5 NA     -0.05      0.05        1.57147728        1.29870349         1.84425108 #> 6 NA     -0.05      0.05        0.13311542       -0.01990612         0.28613695 #>   crosses_baseline_sign significant_at_level error #> 1                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> #> 2                 FALSE                 TRUE  <NA> #> 3                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> #> 4                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> #> 5                 FALSE                 TRUE  <NA> #> 6                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> spliv_tipping_point(path) #> (Intercept)           x           w  #>           0          NA           0  #> attr(,"message") #>                                             (Intercept)  #>              "Baseline interval already includes zero."  #>                                                       x  #> "No zero crossing occurred on the supplied delta grid."  #>                                                       w  #>              "Baseline interval already includes zero."

title: Getting started with spliv
text: BPE uses outcome-independent subset specified examining outcome analysis. Validation reports eligibility diagnostics; search across candidate subgroups. bpe_explore_subsets() available transparent exploratory diagnostics, exploratory subgroup search selecting first passing rule confirmatory BPE. Confirmatory claims require pre-specified design reported validation record.
code: design <- bpe_design(   name = "Theory-defined inactive subset", subset = ~ inactive,   rationale = "The treatment channel is absent in the inactive subset.",   variables_used = "inactive", subset_type = "theory_defined",   pre_specified = TRUE,   transportability_rationale = "The subset direct effect is informative for the target sample." ) validation <- bpe_validate_design(   f, d, design = design, vcov = "hc1",   bpe_min_n_S = 40,   bpe_equiv_margin = 0.25 * sd(resid(lm(x ~ w))) ) validation[c("n_S", "equivalence_passed", "eligibility_passed")] #> $n_S #> [1] 120 #>  #> $equivalence_passed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_passed #> [1] TRUE  # Illustrative synthetic scale only. In a substantive analysis, pre-specify # the equivalence margin from the scientific design rather than tuning it to # obtain BPE eligibility. bpe_margin <- 0.25 * sd(resid(lm(x ~ w))) bpe_fit <- spliv(f, d, method = "bpe", bpe_design = design,                  vcov = "hc1", bpe_min_n_S = 40,                  bpe_equiv_margin = bpe_margin) bpe_fit$estimates #>          term    estimate  std.error    conf.low conf.high #> 1 (Intercept) -0.04159845 0.07364906 -0.18594795 0.1027510 #> 2           x  1.51206522 0.30855850  0.90730168 2.1168288 #> 3           w  0.15414021 0.12487183 -0.09060407 0.3988845

code: @Manual{,   title = {spliv: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects},   author = {Ore Koren},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1},   url = {https://github.com/koren6684/spliv}, }

what: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
text: spliv provides sensitivity analysis instrumental-variables (IV) designs exclusion may fail structured ways, geolocated clustering large data panels. keeps uniform Conley-style uncertainty baseline, adds researcher-specified direct-effect patterns spatial panel data, traces uncertainty sensitivity paths, supports confirmatory Beyond Plausibly Exogenous (BPE) designs based pre-specified instrument-inactive subset. package discover unrestricted direct-effect field: pattern BPE design must justified researcher.

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
code: remotes::install_github("koren6684/spliv")

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
text: examples self-contained use empirical data.
code: library(spliv)  set.seed(42) n <- 240 z <- rnorm(n) w <- rnorm(n) inactive <- seq_len(n) <= n / 2 x <- ifelse(inactive, 0, 1) * z + 0.4 * w + rnorm(n) y <- 1.2 * x + 0.25 * w + 0.15 * z + rnorm(n) d <- data.frame(y, x, z, w, inactive) f <- y ~ x + w | z + w  baseline <- spliv(f, d, method = "uci", delta = 0, vcov = "hc1") baseline$estimates

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
text: Uniform UCI varies excluded instrument’s direct effect bounded interval.
code: uniform <- spliv(   f, d, method = "uci", delta = 0.20, vcov = "hc1",   grid = list(steps = 11) ) uniform$estimates

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
text: Use theory-motivated spliv_pattern() allow direct effects vary observed exposure.
code: pattern <- spliv_pattern(   name = "Exposure pattern",   pattern = ~ w,   rationale = "The alternative channel is expected to be stronger at higher exposure.",   variables_used = "w",   pattern_type = "theory_defined",   normalize = "max_abs" )  patterned_uci <- spliv(   f, d, method = "uci", delta = 0.20, vcov = "hc1",   violation_pattern = pattern, grid = list(steps = 11) ) patterned_ltz <- spliv(   f, d, method = "ltz", delta = 0.20, vcov = "hc1",   violation_pattern = pattern )

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
code: path <- spliv_sensitivity_path(   f, d, method = "uci", delta_grid = seq(0, 0.30, by = 0.05),   vcov = "hc1", violation_pattern = pattern ) head(path) spliv_tipping_point(path) plot(path, term = "x")

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
text: BPE begins outcome-independent, pre-specified design validates estimation. bpe_explore_subsets() deprecated bpe_find_subset() exploratory diagnostics. Searching across subgroups reporting first passing rule confirmatory BPE. Confirmatory BPE requires pre-specified bpe_design() substantive rationale transportability statement.
code: design <- bpe_design(   name = "Theory-defined inactive subset",   subset = ~ inactive,   rationale = "The treatment channel is absent in the inactive subset.",   variables_used = "inactive",   subset_type = "theory_defined",   pre_specified = TRUE,   transportability_rationale = "The subset direct effect is informative for the target sample." )  validation <- bpe_validate_design(   f, d, design = design, vcov = "hc1",   bpe_min_n_S = 40,   bpe_equiv_margin = 0.25 * sd(resid(lm(x ~ w))) ) validation[c("n_S", "equivalence_passed", "eligibility_passed")]  # This is a scale-aware illustrative margin for the synthetic example. In a # substantive analysis, pre-specify the margin from the scientific design; do # not tune it to make BPE pass. bpe_margin <- 0.25 * sd(resid(lm(x ~ w))) bpe_fit <- spliv(   f, d, method = "bpe", bpe_design = design,   vcov = "hc1", bpe_min_n_S = 40, bpe_equiv_margin = bpe_margin ) bpe_fit$estimates

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV with Fixed Effects
text: Reproducibility repository: koren6684/spliv-reproducibility (prospective URL) Citation: see CITATION.cff citation("spliv") public reproducibility repository contains synthetic simulation workflows data-gated Koren (2018) Lelkes, Sood, Iyengar (2017) replication runners. Restricted third-party data redistributed.

what: Create a Confirmatory BPE Design Object — bpe_design
title: Create a Confirmatory BPE Design Object — bpe_design
text: Creates researcher-specified design object confirmatory BPE. subset must justified outside outcome analysis represent theoretically motivated instrument-inactive subset.

title: Create a Confirmatory BPE Design Object — bpe_design
code: bpe_design(   name,   subset,   rationale,   variables_used = NULL,   subset_type = NULL,   pre_specified = TRUE,   transportability_rationale = NULL,   notes = NULL )

title: Create a Confirmatory BPE Design Object — bpe_design
text: name Short design name. subset Subset specification. Accepts one-sided formula, function(data), logical vector length nrow(data), character string naming logical column data. rationale Non-empty substantive justification subset. variables_used Optional character vector describing design variables used define subset. required function-based subsets unless package can safely infer variables. subset_type Optional short label "theory_defined" "design_based". pre_specified Logical indicator confirmatory use. Confirmatory BPE requires TRUE. transportability_rationale Optional description direct effect learned subset may informative target sample. notes Optional free-form notes.

title: Create a Confirmatory BPE Design Object — bpe_design
text: object class "spliv_bpe_design".

title: Create a Confirmatory BPE Design Object — bpe_design
code: design <- bpe_design(   "Inactive subset", ~ inactive,   rationale = "The treatment channel is absent in this subset.",   variables_used = "inactive", pre_specified = TRUE ) bpe_eval_subset(design, data.frame(inactive = c(TRUE, FALSE))) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE #> attr(,"bpe_warnings") #> character(0) #> attr(,"bpe_na_share") #> [1] 0

what: Evaluate a Confirmatory BPE Subset — bpe_eval_subset
title: Evaluate a Confirmatory BPE Subset — bpe_eval_subset
text: Evaluates bpe_design() object data frame returns resulting logical indicator.

title: Evaluate a Confirmatory BPE Subset — bpe_eval_subset
code: bpe_eval_subset(design, data, max_na_share = 0.05)

title: Evaluate a Confirmatory BPE Subset — bpe_eval_subset
text: design bpe_design() object. data Data frame used evaluation. max_na_share Maximum allowable share NA values subset indicator evaluation fails. default 0.05.

title: Evaluate a Confirmatory BPE Subset — bpe_eval_subset
text: logical vector length nrow(data).

title: Evaluate a Confirmatory BPE Subset — bpe_eval_subset
code: design <- bpe_design("Inactive", ~ inactive,   rationale = "The treatment channel is absent.") bpe_eval_subset(design, data.frame(inactive = c(TRUE, FALSE))) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE #> attr(,"bpe_warnings") #> character(0) #> attr(,"bpe_na_share") #> [1] 0

title: Explore Candidate BPE Subsets — bpe_explore_subsets
text: Explores user-supplied candidate subset definitions reports diagnostics transparent exploratory work. function valid confirmatory BPE . Confirmatory BPE requires pre-specified bpe_design() object supplied spliv() bpe_validate_design().

title: Explore Candidate BPE Subsets — bpe_explore_subsets
code: bpe_explore_subsets(   data,   spec,   rules = NULL,   seed = 1,   min_n_S = NULL,   max_F_S = NULL,   min_varZ_S = NULL,   return_all = TRUE )

title: Explore Candidate BPE Subsets — bpe_explore_subsets
text: data Data frame used estimation. spec Either IV formula (y ~ X | Z) list least formula, optional fe, fe_engine, z_names. rules Candidate subset definitions. candidate may one-sided formula evaluated data, function(data) returning logical vector, character string naming logical column data, logical vector length nrow(data), named list components name subset. seed Integer seed deterministic rule evaluation. min_n_S Optional exploratory screen subset size. max_F_S Deprecated exploratory screen first-stage F-statistic. first-stage F-statistic reported diagnostic . min_varZ_S Optional exploratory screen within-subset residualized instrument variance. return_all TRUE (default), returns candidates diagnostics. FALSE, returns first candidate user-supplied order. automatic subset search confirmatory selection performed.

title: Explore Candidate BPE Subsets — bpe_explore_subsets
text: return_all = TRUE, data frame one row per rule list column subset. return_all = FALSE, list first supplied subset, rule, diagnostics.

title: Explore Candidate BPE Subsets — bpe_explore_subsets
text: Warning: function exploratory. used confirmatory BPE inference. Confirmatory BPE requires pre-specified bpe_design() object substantive rationale.

title: Explore Candidate BPE Subsets — bpe_explore_subsets
code: d <- data.frame(y = rnorm(40), x = rnorm(40), z = rnorm(40),   inactive = rep(c(TRUE, FALSE), each = 20)) suppressWarnings(bpe_explore_subsets(   d, y ~ x | z, rules = list(inactive = ~ inactive))) #>       rule candidate_type available n_S share_S   varZ_S       F_S screen_n_ok #> 1 inactive        formula      TRUE  20     0.5 1.027887 0.9819404          NA #>   screen_varZ_ok screen_F_ok message       subset #> 1             NA          NA         TRUE, TR....

what: Deprecated Exploratory BPE Subset Search — bpe_find_subset
title: Deprecated Exploratory BPE Subset Search — bpe_find_subset
text: bpe_find_subset() retained backward compatibility . Use bpe_explore_subsets() exploratory work convert theory-justified subset confirmatory bpe_design() object calling spliv().

code: bpe_find_subset(   data,   spec,   rules = NULL,   seed = 1,   min_n_S = NULL,   max_F_S = NULL,   min_varZ_S = NULL,   return_all = TRUE )

title: Deprecated Exploratory BPE Subset Search — bpe_find_subset
text: data Data frame used estimation. spec Either IV formula (y ~ X | Z) list least formula, optional fe, fe_engine, z_names. rules Candidate subset definitions. candidate may one-sided formula evaluated data, function(data) returning logical vector, character string naming logical column data, logical vector length nrow(data), named list components name subset. seed Integer seed deterministic rule evaluation. min_n_S Optional exploratory screen subset size. max_F_S Deprecated exploratory screen first-stage F-statistic. first-stage F-statistic reported diagnostic . min_varZ_S Optional exploratory screen within-subset residualized instrument variance. return_all TRUE (default), returns candidates diagnostics. FALSE, returns first candidate user-supplied order. automatic subset search confirmatory selection performed.

text: See bpe_explore_subsets().

code: d <- data.frame(y = rnorm(40), x = rnorm(40), z = rnorm(40),   inactive = rep(c(TRUE, FALSE), each = 20)) suppressWarnings(bpe_find_subset(   d, y ~ x | z, rules = list(inactive = ~ inactive))) #>       rule candidate_type available n_S share_S    varZ_S       F_S screen_n_ok #> 1 inactive        formula      TRUE  20     0.5 0.8294879 0.3497427          NA #>   screen_varZ_ok screen_F_ok message       subset #> 1             NA          NA         TRUE, TR....

what: Validate a Confirmatory BPE Design — bpe_validate_design
title: Validate a Confirmatory BPE Design — bpe_validate_design
text: Runs confirmatory BPE eligibility diagnostics pre-specified design without fitting final SPLIV model.

title: Validate a Confirmatory BPE Design — bpe_validate_design
code: bpe_validate_design(   formula,   data,   design,   fe = NULL,   fe_engine = c("fixest", "lfe"),   vcov = c("iid", "hc1", "cluster"),   cluster = NULL,   z_names = NULL,   bpe_min_n_S = 2000,   bpe_min_clusters_S = 30,   bpe_min_varZ_S = 1e-06,   bpe_equiv_margin,   bpe_equiv_level = 0.95,   bpe_transport = c("none", "sampling", "conservative"),   bpe_transport_kappa = 0,   bpe_kappa = 1,   scale_instrument = c("residual_sd", "none") )

title: Validate a Confirmatory BPE Design — bpe_validate_design
text: formula IV formula y ~ X | Z. data Data frame. design bpe_design() object. fe Optional one-sided formula fixed effects. fe_engine FE demeaning engine, one "fixest" "lfe". vcov One "iid", "hc1", "cluster". cluster Cluster ids one-sided formula vcov = "cluster". z_names Optional instrument name BPE. Confirmatory BPE currently supports exactly one instrument. bpe_min_n_S Minimum subset size threshold. bpe_min_clusters_S Minimum number clusters required subset clustered covariance used. bpe_min_varZ_S Minimum residualized instrument variance required subset. bpe_equiv_margin Researcher-specified equivalence margin first-stage coefficient. Eligibility based first-stage equivalence interval, first-stage F-statistic. bpe_equiv_level Confidence level used first-stage equivalence interval. bpe_transport One "none", "sampling", "conservative". Transportability assumption reflected reported covariance; established subset . bpe_transport_kappa Non-negative scalar controlling conservative transport covariance inflation. bpe_kappa Positive scalar multiplier applied transported BPE covariance embedded LTZ prior. scale_instrument One "residual_sd" "none".

title: Validate a Confirmatory BPE Design — bpe_validate_design
text: object class "spliv_bpe_validation" containing design metadata, subset diagnostics, first-stage equivalence diagnostics, reduced- form direct-effect estimates, covariance components confirmatory BPE.

title: Validate a Confirmatory BPE Design — bpe_validate_design
code: set.seed(2) d <- data.frame(   y = rnorm(80), x = rnorm(80), z = rnorm(80),   inactive = rep(c(TRUE, FALSE), each = 40) ) design <- bpe_design("Inactive", ~ inactive,   rationale = "The treatment channel is absent.") bpe_validate_design(y ~ x | z, d, design,   bpe_min_n_S = 20, bpe_equiv_margin = 1) #> $design_name #> [1] "Inactive" #>  #> $rationale #> [1] "The treatment channel is absent." #>  #> $subset_type #> NULL #>  #> $variables_used #> [1] "inactive" #>  #> $pre_specified #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $transportability_rationale #> NULL #>  #> $notes #> NULL #>  #> $n_S #> [1] 40 #>  #> $share_S #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $G_S #> NULL #>  #> $varZ_S #>         z  #> 0.8737843  #>  #> $residualized_instrument_sd_S #>         z  #> 0.9347643  #>  #> $residualized_instrument_sd #>        z  #> 1.031161  #>  #> $residualized_treatment_sd_S #>        x  #> 1.150203  #>  #> $first_stage_coefficient #>         z  #> 0.2973111  #>  #> $first_stage_se #>         z  #> 0.1936949  #>  #> $first_stage_ci #>         lower     upper #> z -0.08232386 0.6769461 #>  #> $first_stage_f_statistic #>        z  #> 2.356058  #>  #> $first_stage_f_type #> [1] "conventional_ols_diagnostic" #>  #> $first_stage_effect_one_residual_sd_Z #>         z  #> 0.2779158  #>  #> $standardized_first_stage_effect #>         z  #> 0.2416232  #>  #> $equivalence_margin #> z  #> 1  #>  #> $equivalence_level #> [1] 0.95 #>  #> $equivalence_passed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_passed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_checks #> $eligibility_checks$pre_specified #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_checks$rationale #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_checks$minimum_n #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_checks$minimum_clusters #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_checks$residual_variation #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $eligibility_checks$equivalence #> [1] TRUE #>  #>  #> $reduced_form_direct_effect #>          z  #> -0.2241848  #>  #> $reduced_form_direct_effect_cov #>            z #> z 0.03586035 #> title: Demean with fixest — demean_fixest
text: Demeans vectors/matrices high-dimensional fixed effects using fixest::demean.

what: spliv: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV — spliv-package
title: spliv: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV — spliv-package
text: Matrix-based patterned sensitivity analysis plausibly exogenous IV inference scalable fixed-effect residualization, researcher-specified direct-effect patterns, sensitivity paths delta, optional confirmatory BPE diagnostics.

title: spliv: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for IV — spliv-package
text: Maintainer: Ore Koren okoren@iu.edu

what: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
text: Main estimator patterned sensitivity analysis exclusion violations fixed-effect residualized IV designs.

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
code: plausexog(   formula,   data,   fe = NULL,   fe_engine = c("fixest", "lfe"),   vcov = c("iid", "hc1", "cluster"),   cluster = NULL,   method = c("ltz", "uci", "bpe"),   prior = NULL,   delta = NULL,   violation_pattern = NULL,   bpe = FALSE,   bpe_design = NULL,   bpe_spec = list(design = NULL, subset = NULL, z_names = NULL),   bpe_kappa = 1,   bpe_omega = NULL,   bpe_min_n_S = 2000,   bpe_min_clusters_S = 30,   bpe_max_F_S = NULL,   bpe_min_varZ_S = 1e-06,   bpe_equiv_margin = NULL,   bpe_equiv_level = 0.95,   bpe_transport = c("sampling", "none", "conservative"),   bpe_transport_kappa = 0,   bpe_not_applicable = c("na", "error"),   scale_instrument = c("residual_sd", "none"),   grid = list(),   ... )  plausexog_iv(...)  spliv(   formula,   data,   fe = NULL,   fe_engine = c("fixest", "lfe"),   vcov = c("iid", "hc1", "cluster"),   cluster = NULL,   method = c("ltz", "uci", "bpe"),   prior = NULL,   delta = NULL,   violation_pattern = NULL,   bpe = FALSE,   bpe_design = NULL,   bpe_spec = list(design = NULL, subset = NULL, z_names = NULL),   bpe_kappa = 1,   bpe_omega = NULL,   bpe_min_n_S = 2000,   bpe_min_clusters_S = 30,   bpe_max_F_S = NULL,   bpe_min_varZ_S = 1e-06,   bpe_equiv_margin = NULL,   bpe_equiv_level = 0.95,   bpe_transport = c("sampling", "none", "conservative"),   bpe_transport_kappa = 0,   bpe_not_applicable = c("na", "error"),   scale_instrument = c("residual_sd", "none"),   grid = list(),   ... )

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
text: formula IV formula y ~ X | Z. data Data frame. fe Optional one-sided formula fixed effects. fe_engine FE demeaning engine, one "fixest" "lfe". vcov One "iid", "hc1", "cluster". cluster Cluster ids one-sided formula, required vcov = "cluster". method One "ltz", "uci", "bpe". prior Optional prior list mu Omega (omega) LTZ. violation_pattern supplied, patterned LTZ currently requires scalar prior pattern coefficient. delta Optional non-negative scalar sensitivity magnitude. method = "uci", delta implies theta bounds [-delta, +delta] unless explicit bounds supplied grid. method = "ltz" explicit prior, delta induces zero-mean normal LTZ prior. violation_pattern Optional spliv_pattern() object describing direct effect instrument may vary across observations. omitted, LTZ/UCI retain package's backward-compatible uniform direct-effect behavior. argument currently supported LTZ UCI, confirmatory BPE. bpe Logical; TRUE, learn prior moments confirmatory bpe_design() run LTZ. bpe_design Optional bpe_design() object confirmatory BPE. bpe_spec Optional list. Prefer bpe_spec = list(design = my_design). backward compatibility, bpe_spec$subset may also supplied, must represent explicit researcher-supplied subset paired non-empty rationale, explicit pre_specified = TRUE, relevant metadata variables_used subset_type. Supply exactly one confirmatory subset source: bpe_design, bpe_spec$design, bpe_spec$subset. Exploratory subset_rule inputs accepted confirmatory estimation. bpe_kappa Positive scalar multiplier applied confirmatory BPE covariance transport adjustment. bpe_omega Deprecated. Confirmatory BPE now uses full reduced-form covariance subset together bpe_transport. bpe_min_n_S Minimum subset size required BPE eligibility. Default 2000. bpe_min_clusters_S Minimum number clusters required subset S vcov = "cluster". Default 30. bpe_max_F_S Deprecated. first-stage F-statistic reported diagnostics longer determines confirmatory BPE eligibility. bpe_min_varZ_S Minimum residualized instrument variance required subset S. Default 1e-6. bpe_equiv_margin Researcher-specified first-stage equivalence margin. Confirmatory BPE currently supports exactly one instrument. bpe_equiv_level Confidence level first-stage equivalence check. bpe_transport One "none", "sampling", "conservative". bpe_transport_kappa Non-negative scalar controlling conservative transport covariance inflation. bpe_not_applicable Behavior subset diagnostics fail. One "na" (default) return NA estimates, "error" stop. scale_instrument One "residual_sd" (default) "none". grid List controlling UCI bounds tuning parameters. backward-compatible scalar UCI, grid$delta supplied grid$gmin/grid$gmax omitted, package interprets delta direct-effect bound [-delta, +delta] chosen scale_instrument. violation_pattern supplied, grid$delta instead refers theta bounds pattern-scaled direct effect. ... Reserved.

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
text: Object class plausexog_fit.

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
text: spliv implements patterned sensitivity analysis exclusion violations IV designs fixed effects residualization steps. Researchers can supply theoretically motivated spliv_pattern() object specify direct effects instrument expected larger smaller, package scales LTZ/UCI sensitivity along pattern. package estimate unrestricted direct-effect field. Instead, researchers supply structured pattern ask whether conclusions survive direct effects scaled along pattern. applied work, users usually vary delta range spliv_sensitivity_path() rather report one arbitrary sensitivity value. Patterned sensitivity currently supports one endogenous treatment, one excluded instrument, one researcher-specified pattern time. Confirmatory BPE subgroup-search procedure. researcher must supply pre-specified bpe_design() object, package validates subset, BPE proceeds confirmatory eligibility checks pass. first-stage F-statistic still reported diagnostics, confirmatory BPE eligibility determined pre-specification checks, subset size, cluster count, residualized instrument variation, first-stage equivalence interval. BPE reduced-form covariance propagated full covariance matrix can optionally inflated via bpe_transport.

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
text: plausexog() plausexog_iv() deprecated compatibility aliases spliv().

title: Patterned Sensitivity Analysis for Plausibly Exogenous IV — spliv
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(y = rnorm(60), x = rnorm(60), z = rnorm(60), w = rnorm(60)) fit <- spliv(y ~ x + w | z + w, d, method = "uci", delta = 0.1) fit$estimates #>          term    conf.low conf.high #> 1 (Intercept)  -0.8544988  1.363666 #> 2           x -10.1507140  7.661939 #> 3           w  -0.5514807  0.410585

title: Evaluate a Direct-Effect Pattern — spliv_eval_pattern
code: d <- data.frame(exposure = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 5)) p <- spliv_pattern("Exposure", ~ exposure,   rationale = "Illustration of a monotone exposure pattern.") spliv_eval_pattern(p, d) #> [1] -1.0 -0.5  0.0  0.5  1.0 #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_raw_summary") #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_raw_summary")$n #> [1] 5 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_raw_summary")$mean #> [1] 0 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_raw_summary")$sd #> [1] 0.7905694 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_raw_summary")$min #> [1] -1 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_raw_summary")$max #> [1] 1 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_summary") #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_summary")$n #> [1] 5 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_summary")$mean #> [1] 0 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_summary")$sd #> [1] 0.7905694 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_summary")$min #> [1] -1 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_summary")$max #> [1] 1 #>  #> attr(,"spliv_pattern_warnings") #> character(0)

what: Create a Patterned Exclusion-Violation Object — spliv_pattern
title: Create a Patterned Exclusion-Violation Object — spliv_pattern
text: Creates researcher-specified direct-effect pattern patterned sensitivity analysis. pattern determines direct effects instrument expected larger smaller estimation sample.

title: Create a Patterned Exclusion-Violation Object — spliv_pattern
code: spliv_pattern(   name,   pattern,   rationale,   variables_used = NULL,   pattern_type = NULL,   normalize = c("max_abs", "sd", "none"),   center = FALSE,   pre_specified = TRUE,   notes = NULL )

title: Create a Patterned Exclusion-Violation Object — spliv_pattern
text: name Short pattern name. pattern Pattern specification. Accepts one-sided formula, function(data), numeric vector length nrow(data), logical vector length nrow(data), character string naming numeric logical column data. rationale Substantive justification proposed pattern. variables_used Optional character vector describing variables used define pattern. pattern_type Optional short label "theory_defined" "design_based". normalize One "max_abs" (default), "sd", "none". center Logical; TRUE, subtracts estimation-sample mean normalization. pre_specified Logical indicator whether pattern chosen examining outcome results. notes Optional free-form notes.

title: Create a Patterned Exclusion-Violation Object — spliv_pattern
text: object class "spliv_pattern".

title: Create a Patterned Exclusion-Violation Object — spliv_pattern
code: p <- spliv_pattern(   name = "Exposure", pattern = ~ exposure,   rationale = "The alternative channel follows exposure.",   variables_used = "exposure", pattern_type = "theory_defined" ) p$name #> [1] "Exposure"

what: Sensitivity Path over Delta Grid — spliv_sensitivity_path
title: Sensitivity Path over Delta Grid — spliv_sensitivity_path
text: Runs spliv() repeatedly user-supplied delta_grid returns tidy sensitivity-path object. recommended workflow patterned uniform sensitivity analysis: users usually report intervals change range delta values rather selecting one arbitrary sensitivity level.

title: Sensitivity Path over Delta Grid — spliv_sensitivity_path
code: spliv_sensitivity_path(   formula,   data,   method = c("uci", "ltz"),   delta_grid = seq(0, 0.2, by = 0.01),   violation_pattern = NULL,   stop_on_error = TRUE,   ... )

title: Sensitivity Path over Delta Grid — spliv_sensitivity_path
text: formula IV formula y ~ X | Z. data Data frame. method One "uci" "ltz". spliv_sensitivity_path() intentionally excludes confirmatory BPE. delta_grid Non-negative sensitivity grid. UCI, value d implies theta bounds [-d, +d]. violation_pattern Optional spliv_pattern() object. omitted, path uses package's backward-compatible uniform direct-effect pattern. stop_on_error Logical; TRUE (default), stop first failed fit. FALSE, record NA rows store error message returned error column. ... Additional named arguments passed spliv(), fe, vcov, cluster, scale_instrument, grid = list(level = 0.95).

title: Sensitivity Path over Delta Grid — spliv_sensitivity_path
text: data frame class c("spliv_sensitivity_path", "data.frame"). returned object includes path columns delta, method, estimate, conf_low, conf_high, contains_zero, pattern metadata, plus attributes containing original call, supplied grid, tipping-point summary.

title: Sensitivity Path over Delta Grid — spliv_sensitivity_path
code: set.seed(5) d <- data.frame(y = rnorm(80), x = rnorm(80), z = rnorm(80)) p <- spliv_sensitivity_path(y ~ x | z, d, method = "uci",   delta_grid = c(0, 0.1), vcov = "hc1") head(p) #>          term delta method  estimate    conf_low conf_high contains_zero #> 1 (Intercept)   0.0    uci  0.108665  -0.4663013 0.6836313          TRUE #> 2           x   0.0    uci -1.979595  -6.9028208 2.9436307          TRUE #> 3 (Intercept)   0.1    uci  0.108665  -0.6973215 1.0299906          TRUE #> 4           x   0.1    uci -1.979595 -10.4825196 4.4046121          TRUE #>            pattern_name pattern_type violation_pattern_used scale_instrument #> 1 Uniform direct effect      uniform                  FALSE      residual_sd #> 2 Uniform direct effect      uniform                  FALSE      residual_sd #> 3 Uniform direct effect      uniform                  FALSE      residual_sd #> 4 Uniform direct effect      uniform                  FALSE      residual_sd #>   nobs se theta_min theta_max baseline_estimate baseline_conf_low #> 1   80 NA       0.0       0.0          0.108665        -0.4663013 #> 2   80 NA       0.0       0.0         -1.979595        -6.9028208 #> 3   80 NA      -0.1       0.1          0.108665        -0.4663013 #> 4   80 NA      -0.1       0.1         -1.979595        -6.9028208 #>   baseline_conf_high crosses_baseline_sign significant_at_level error #> 1          0.6836313                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> #> 2          2.9436307                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> #> 3          0.6836313                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA> #> 4          2.9436307                  TRUE                FALSE  <NA>

what: Extract Tipping-Point Delta from a Sensitivity Path — spliv_tipping_point
title: Extract Tipping-Point Delta from a Sensitivity Path — spliv_tipping_point
text: Returns tipping-point attribute stored spliv_sensitivity_path() object. term, tipping point smallest delta supplied grid whose interval includes zero.

title: Extract Tipping-Point Delta from a Sensitivity Path — spliv_tipping_point
code: spliv_tipping_point(x)

title: Extract Tipping-Point Delta from a Sensitivity Path — spliv_tipping_point
text: x spliv_sensitivity_path object.

title: Extract Tipping-Point Delta from a Sensitivity Path — spliv_tipping_point
text: Named numeric vector tipping-point values.

title: Extract Tipping-Point Delta from a Sensitivity Path — spliv_tipping_point
code: set.seed(6) d <- data.frame(y = rnorm(80), x = rnorm(80), z = rnorm(80)) p <- spliv_sensitivity_path(y ~ x | z, d, method = "uci", delta_grid = c(0, 0.1)) spliv_tipping_point(p) #> (Intercept)           x  #>           0           0  #> attr(,"message") #>                                (Intercept)  #> "Baseline interval already includes zero."  #>                                          x  #> "Baseline interval already includes zero."

title: spliv 0.1.0
text: Initial public staging release. version provides uniform patterned UCI/LTZ sensitivity analysis, sensitivity paths tipping points, fixed-effect residualization, confirmatory BPE design/validation, compatibility wrappers earlier plausibly-exogenous API.
